Film: dramatizes the Battle of the Ice; a Teutonic Order bishop wears a hat with a swastika; restored the director's political reputation

front: Film: abandoned after 50 hours of footage; Upton Sinclair financed it at Chaplin's request; intended to cover the entire history of Mexico
Film: Part II condemned as 'anti-historical' by the Central Committee; the director died of a heart attack in 1948 after refusing to revise it
Director: developed five types of montage — metric, rhythmic, tonal, overtonal, intellectual — with peers like Vsevolod Pudovkin
Director: Film Form essay collection argues 'montage is conflict'; essay on cinema's 'fourth dimension' analyzes haiku and kabuki
Director: wrote an essay arguing Charles Dickens was the strongest influence on D.W. Griffith
Director: early career — staged Enough Stupidity in Every Wise Man using commedia dell'arte tropes
